title: Museum Environmental Control Type A1
summary: Long-term outer limits for temperature and relative humidity in museums, galleries, archives, and libraries with precision control and no seasonal changes to relative humidity.

title: Museum Environmental Control Type A2
summary: Long-term outer limits for temperature and relative humidity in museums, galleries, archives, and libraries with precision control and seasonal changes in temperature only.

title: Museum Environmental Control Type B
summary: Long-term outer limits for temperature and relative humidity in museums, galleries, archives, and libraries with limited control and seasonal changes in relative humidity and large seasonal changes in temperature.

title: Museum Environmental Control Type C
summary: Long-term outer limits for temperature and relative humidity in museums, galleries, archives, and libraries to prevent relative humidity extremes and high temperature extremes.

title: Museum Environmental Control Type D
summary: Long-term outer limit for relative humidity in museums, galleries, archives, and libraries in open structured buildings or historic houses to prevent very high relative humidity (dampness).

title: Cool Storage for Chemically Unstable Organic Materials
summary: Temperature and relative humidity specifications for cool storage of chemically unstable organic materials in modern purpose-built buildings or rooms.

title: Cold Storage for Chemically Unstable Organic Materials
summary: Temperature and relative humidity specifications for cold storage of chemically unstable organic materials in modern purpose-built buildings or rooms.

title: Frozen Storage for Chemically Unstable Organic Materials
summary: Temperature and relative humidity specifications for frozen storage of chemically unstable organic materials in modern purpose-built buildings or rooms.

title: Maximum Pollutant Concentrations for General Collection
summary: Maximum average pollutant concentrations allowed for a general collection (excluding moderate- and high-risk objects) to prevent low-level damage for at least 1 year.
evidence_quote: should stay below 1000 μg/m3 (400 ppb) for acetic acid; 1 μg/m3 (0.7 ppb) for hydrogen sulfide; and 10 μg/m3 for nitrogen dioxide (5 ppb), ozone (5 ppb), and fine particles.

title: Basic Level Pollutant Control Strategy
summary: Basic level control strategy for airborne pollutants in buildings with HVAC systems and display cases/storage cabinets/boxes.
evidence_quote: Provide basic fine-particulate filtration such as that recommended for office space regulation or for LEED certification (EQc 5.1).

title: Intermediate Level Pollutant Control Strategy
summary: Intermediate level control strategy for airborne pollutants in buildings with HVAC systems and display cases/storage cabinets/boxes.
evidence_quote: Use medium-efficiency fine-particulate filtration or select filter performance based on outdoor concentration provided by local authority.

title: Advanced Level Pollutant Control Strategy
summary: Advanced level control strategy for airborne pollutants in buildings with HVAC systems and display cases/storage cabinets/boxes.
evidence_quote: Use high-efficiency fine-particulate filtration or select filter performance based on risk analysis result.

title: Corrosion Coupon Flow Velocity
summary: Recommended flow velocity for corrosion coupon testing in water systems to avoid erosion effects.
evidence_quote: velocity of 1 m/s is normally used, and velocities in excess of 1.5 m/s may influence erosion, particularly of soft metals such as copper.

title: Legionella Optimum Growth Temperature
summary: Optimum temperature range for Legionella bacteria growth in water systems.
evidence_quote: The optimum temperature range for growth is 32 to 41°C.

title: Copper and Silver Ion Concentrations for Microbial Control
summary: Concentrations of copper and silver ions released into water for microbial population control.
evidence_quote: generate 0.4 to 0.8 mg/L of copper and/or 40 to 80 μg/L of silver.
